vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF for #727: codify orchestrator role-separation (implementation agent vs Phase 6 polling sub-agent) plus a canonical poller-prompt template artifact carrying the proven prompt body and parsing fixes. Stops the recurring orchestrator failure where post-PR work is bundled into an implementation-agent prompt that exits at PR-open via succeeded lifecycle, AND the recurring poll-script parsing bugs (markdown-link `Last reviewed commit:` + raw `\b(P0|P1)\b` substring scan with negation false-positive). Per-[AXIOM] encoding stack: Tier 4 RFC2119 rules in skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md Phase 6 Step 1, Tier 1 deterministic template at templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md, Tier 1 content tests in tests/content/test_skills.py, Tier 5 cross-reference in meta/lessons.md.

plan.title: Codify orchestrator role-separation + canonical poller-prompt template (per [AXIOM])

narratives.Problem: Recurring orchestrator failure: implementation sub-agents launched via `start_agent` are given prompts that conflate two distinct roles defined in `skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md` -- the implementation-agent role (Phases 0-5: do the discrete work, open PR, exit) and the Phase 6 polling-sub-agent role (short-lived poller that watches Greptile / CI / external events and messages the parent). Implementation agents that are told to 'watch for Greptile' exit at PR-open via the `succeeded` lifecycle and the watch never starts; bugs of this shape have only been masked by sleep-timing coincidences. Independently, two parsing bugs in hand-authored Greptile poll scripts have repeated across the post-#721 swarm (Agent D, PR #726 P2 cleanup): (a) the `Last reviewed commit:` SHA-extraction regex assumed inline SHA but Greptile emits a markdown link `[<title>](<commit-url>)` so the regex never matched and the poller fell through every iteration; (b) raw `\b(P0|P1)\b` substring scan over the body false-positived on Greptile's clean-summary phrasing 'No P0 or P1 issues found'. A second-comment update to #727 surfaced an adjacent anti-pattern in the same family: sub-agent prompts encouraged chaining `rm` / `Remove-Item` with `git commit` / `git push` (e.g. the canonical UTF-8-safe commit-message pattern with tail-cleanup), which poisons Warp's `is_risky` classification and forces user approval on every otherwise-safe operation.
narratives.Overview: Per the `## Rule Authority [AXIOM]` block in `main.md` (deterministic > Taskfile > vBRIEF > RFC2119 > prose) the canonical encoding is a 4-tier stack: (Tier 4 primary) explicit `!` MUST and `âŠ—` MUST NOT rules in `skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md` Phase 6 Step 1 covering role-separation, canonical-template usage, and the rm-chaining hygiene rules; (Tier 1 secondary) `templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md` artifact carrying the proven prompt body with placeholders `{pr_number}`, `{repo}`, `{poll_interval_seconds}`, `{poll_cap_minutes}`, `{parent_agent_id}` and encoding both parsing fixes (markdown-link regex for `Last reviewed commit:` and badge-based / negation-aware findings detection); (Tier 1 enforcement) regression tests in `tests/content/test_skills.py` asserting template existence, all five placeholders, both parsing-fix tokens, SKILL cross-reference to the template, and stable-substring presence of every `!` rule and `âŠ—` anti-pattern added in this PR; (Tier 5 tertiary) one-paragraph cross-reference in `meta/lessons.md` pointing at the SKILL section + template path -- prose is fallback only per [AXIOM] so the lesson does NOT duplicate the rule body. A Taskfile target was considered as the secondary encoding but rejected: any Taskfile invocation runs synchronously inside the parent's own tool call, locking up the conversation while the watch is pending. The only Warp runtime surface with a true async callback channel is `start_agent` + messaging.
narratives.Background: Three identical mistakes by the same orchestrator across the 2026-04-28 v0.21.0 cut session (#721): PR #722 sub-agent (worked by accident via `time.sleep(180)` coincidence), PR #726 sub-agent (Agent 2 finished its final report ~30s before Greptile posted, exited via `succeeded` without seeing the review), and PR #727 sub-agent (same instruction shape). Plus the post-#721 swarm follow-up surfaced (a) the rm-chaining anti-pattern (Agent 1 hit four consecutive approval prompts on chained `rm` + `git commit` / `git push` patterns) and (b) the two poll-script parsing bugs above on PR #726 P2 cleanup, where Agent D's poller ran past its expected exit even though Greptile had been clean for ~16 minutes. This issue closes the gap structurally so any future orchestrator inherits the proven encoding. Precedent: #701 already established the canonical encoding pattern (skill rules + Taskfile + lessons cross-ref tiering) for the closing-keyword Layer 3 false-positive; this issue applies the same pattern to orchestrator role-separation.
narratives.Constraint: MUST stay within the 4-tier encoding stack (no new prose-only rules in `main.md`; per [AXIOM] prose is fallback only). MUST NOT duplicate rule body across tiers (the lessons entry is a cross-reference, not a re-statement). MUST preserve existing skill rules unchanged -- new rules go alongside, not in place of, the existing Phase 6 Step 1 polling-sub-agent contract. MUST keep the canonical template Python-formattable (`prompt.format(pr_number=N, ...)`) so the parent agent uses it via a single read+format step. Files MUST be < 1000 lines (the current `skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md` is at 565 lines; new rules will push it to ~600).
narratives.Risk: Low. The scope is purely additive at the framework level: new template file (no rename / move of existing files), new content tests (parametrized, mirror existing patterns), additive rules in the swarm SKILL Phase 6 Step 1 (no existing-rule deletion or weakening), and a short paragraph appended to `meta/lessons.md`. The dogfood self-test in Stage 9 of the implementation flow uses the new template's parsing logic against THIS PR -- so the template gets exercised end-to-end before merge.
narratives.Outcome: Acceptance: (1) `templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md` exists carrying all 5 placeholders verbatim and both parsing-fix tokens; (2) `skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md` Phase 6 Step 1 carries 5 `!` MUST rules + 4 `âŠ—` MUST NOT anti-patterns covering role-separation, canonical-template usage, rm-chaining hygiene, parent-turn polling prohibition, implementation-agent monitoring instruction prohibition, and pure-poller-default prohibition; (3) `tests/content/test_skills.py` adds regressions asserting template existence, placeholder presence, parsing-fix tokens, SKILL cross-reference, and rule-body presence; (4) `meta/lessons.md` carries a short cross-reference entry; (5) `CHANGELOG.md` `[Unreleased]` entry under Added cites `Closes #727` and summarises the 4-tier encoding stack; (6) `task check` and `task ci:local` pass on the branch. The fix is verified by orchestrating the rest of this PR's review cycle using the new template's parsing logic and observing both that the parent retains conversation steerability and that neither parsing bug regresses.
narratives.Test: Verification: (a) `tests/content/test_skills.py::test_swarm_greptile_poller_prompt_template_exists` asserts the new template file exists; (b) `test_swarm_greptile_poller_prompt_placeholders` asserts all 5 placeholders are present verbatim; (c) `test_swarm_greptile_poller_prompt_parsing_fixes` asserts the markdown-link regex for `Last reviewed commit:` and badge-based / negation-aware findings detection are present in the template body; (d) `test_swarm_skill_references_poller_template` asserts `skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md` references the template path at least once; (e) parametrized `test_swarm_skill_role_separation_rules_present` asserts stable substrings for each of the 5 `!` rules and 4 `âŠ—` anti-patterns; (f) `task check` and `task ci:local` pass green; (g) the dogfood self-test in Stage 9 exercises the template parsing logic end-to-end against this PR's own Greptile review.
narratives.Action: Swarm-scope leaf: independent of any other vBRIEF (touches only `vbrief/proposed/2026-04-29-727-orchestrator-role-separation.vbrief.json`, `skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md`, `templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md` (new), `tests/content/test_skills.py`, `meta/lessons.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`). Closes #727 once merged.

items.title: Add 5 ! MUST rules + 4 âŠ— MUST NOT anti-patterns to skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md Phase 6 Step 1

narrative.Action: Add to Phase 6 Step 1 a new sub-section encoding: (1) post-PR sub-agents MUST embody `skills/deft-directive-review-cycle/SKILL.md` end-to-end as a single coherent role (do not split poll vs fix); (2) post-PR monitoring (Greptile, CI, downloadCount, lifecycle events) MUST spawn a fresh poller sub-agent via `start_agent`, parent yields with no tool calls; (3) when delegating to a poller MUST use the canonical template at `templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md` with placeholders filled (do not hand-author per-watch prompts); (4) sub-agent prompts MUST instruct the agent to run destructive commands (`rm`, `Remove-Item`, `del`, `git clean`, etc.) in their OWN shell call (never chained with non-destructive commands); (5) when using the canonical PowerShell UTF-8-safe commit-message pattern, MUST NOT clean up the temp file in the same shell call. Anti-patterns: (âŠ—) run a poll loop in the parent's own turn (via task / shell sleep / time.sleep / any synchronous tool call); (âŠ—) bundle 'watch for Greptile' / 'monitor CI' instructions into an implementation agent's `start_agent` prompt -- implementation agents exit at PR-open via succeeded lifecycle; (âŠ—) spawn a 'pure poller' sub-agent for a PR that has likely findings -- pure pollers are appropriate ONLY when no fixes are expected; (âŠ—) chain `rm` (or any destructive command) with `git commit` / `git push` / any non-destructive command in a single shell pipeline.
narrative.Acceptance: All 5 ! rules and 4 âŠ— anti-patterns appear verbatim in Phase 6 Step 1 (or the sibling Anti-Patterns block where appropriate); existing rules unchanged.

items.title: Create templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md carrying the canonical poller-prompt body

narrative.Action: Create `templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md` (new file) with placeholders `{pr_number}`, `{repo}`, `{poll_interval_seconds}`, `{poll_cap_minutes}`, `{parent_agent_id}`. Encode: single-role posture (review-cycle agent embodying `skills/deft-directive-review-cycle/SKILL.md` end-to-end); bounded poll loop (default 90s interval, 30 min cap); markdown-link regex for `Last reviewed commit:` per #727 comment 2 Bug 1; badge-based / negation-aware findings detection per #727 comment 2 Bug 2 (count ` 3 as the clean threshold; three terminal exit conditions with exact message subjects/body templates (CLEAN, NEW P0/P1 FINDINGS, ERRORED, TIMEOUT); explicit 'no more polling, exiting now' suffix as a contract; per AGENTS.md fetch 'Comments Outside Diff' via `gh pr view --comments` with `do_not_summarize_output: true` semantics; constraints section forbidding rm-chaining, parent-turn polling, role-mixing.
narrative.Acceptance: Template parameterizes via `prompt.format(...)` so a parent does `read_files -> .format -> start_agent`; all 5 placeholders verbatim; both parsing-fix tokens present.

narrative.Action: Add to `tests/content/test_skills.py` (mirroring existing parametrized style): (1) `test_swarm_greptile_poller_prompt_template_exists` -- asserts `templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md` is a file; (2) `test_swarm_greptile_poller_prompt_placeholders` -- asserts all 5 placeholders verbatim; (3) `test_swarm_greptile_poller_prompt_parsing_fixes` -- asserts the markdown-link regex form for `Last reviewed commit:` AND the badge-based / negation-aware findings detection; (4) `test_swarm_skill_references_poller_template` -- asserts `skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md` references `templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md` at least once; (5) parametrized `test_swarm_skill_role_separation_rules_present` -- asserts stable substrings for each of the 5 ! rules and 4 âŠ— anti-patterns from item swarm-skill-role-separation-rules.
narrative.Acceptance: All five tests pass under `task check` (pytest); no existing tests regress.

items.title: Append short cross-reference entry to meta/lessons.md (NON-duplicating per [AXIOM])

narrative.Action: Append a short `## Orchestrator Role Separation + Canonical Poller Template (2026-04)` section pointing at `skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md` Phase 6 Step 1 (primary encoding) and `templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md` (template artifact). Single paragraph; cross-references #727 (acceptance issue with full anti-pattern record). Does NOT duplicate the rule body -- per [AXIOM] prose is fallback only.
narrative.Acceptance: Entry appears in `meta/lessons.md` referencing the SKILL section + template path with #727 cite; rule body not duplicated.

narrative.Action: Add a substantial entry under `## [Unreleased] / ### Added` summarising the 4-tier encoding stack (Tier 4 swarm SKILL rules + Tier 1 template artifact + Tier 1 content tests + Tier 5 lessons cross-ref) and the recurrence record from #727 (three identical mistakes in the v0.21.0 cut session, two poll-script parsing bugs surfaced post-#721, the rm-chaining anti-pattern). End with `Closes #727`.
narrative.Acceptance: CHANGELOG.md `## [Unreleased] / ### Added` carries the new entry referencing #727 by Closes; existing entries unchanged.

x-tracking.scope_authority: deft-727-fix orchestrator-skill agent (parent monitor's plan stages 1-10)
